title: ⚠ MAP KEY — these are MODERN political borders
description: All coloured regions are MODERN country borders. Cannabis's cultural geography spans Scythian nomad territory, ancient Persia, Mughal India, colonial Caribbean, Rastafari Jamaica — none of which align with present-day states.

Central Asia (modern borders) — cannabis origin (Hindu Kush / Pamir)
description: Modern political borders — Afghanistan + Pakistan + Tajikistan + Uzbekistan region. Cannabis sativa + indica evolved in the Hindu Kush and Pamir mountains. Scythian and Pazyryk archaeological sites document ritual use ~2,500 years ago.

India (modern borders) — bhang sacramental tradition
description: Modern political border. Bhang documented in Atharva Veda (~1500 BCE). Cannabis is a legal cultural exemption during Hindu festivals (Holi, Maha Shivratri) despite general prohibition.

title: Morocco Rif Mountains (modern borders) — hashish heartland
description: Modern political border. Rif region hashish production 15th century onward; now the largest illicit supplier to European markets. Complex colonial + post-colonial policy history.

Jamaica (modern borders) — Rastafari ganja sacrament
description: Modern political border (post-1962 independence). Indian indentured labourers post-1838 abolition seeded cannabis culture; Rastafari movement (1930s) made ganja a central religious sacrament.

title: USA (modern borders) — patchwork legalization
description: Modern political border. As of 2026: ~24 states recreational, ~38 states medical, federal still Schedule I. 1937 Marihuana Tax Act + 1970 CSA created federal criminalization that fell disproportionately on Black + Latino communities.

title: Canada (modern borders) — nationally legal since 2018
description: Modern political border. First G7 nation to fully legalize recreational cannabis (2018). Federally regulated market ~$4B/year. Exports to Germany, Israel, Australia medical markets.

title: Germany (modern borders) — legalized 2024
description: Modern political border. Germany legalized recreational cannabis in April 2024 — largest European country to do so. Cannabis Social Clubs model regulates supply.
